title: Are all graphs on 9 vertices 1-11-representable?
lean_gate_reason: The exact intended 9-vertex slice still appears open, while the current packet's strongest honest claim is a rediscovered isolated-vertex corollary already implied by canonical Lemma 2.3(a) together with Theorem 3.5. Lean would formalize known support rather than seal a paper-ready packet.
micro_paper_assessment: Fail for the current packet: the strongest honest claim is a structural but known supporting slice, not a frontier title theorem or a publishable theorem slice.
family_affinity: High: the proof is a reusable vertex-reinsertion reduction tied directly to the 9-vertex frontier.
generalization_signal: Positive for adjoining isolated vertices in any order; unresolved for universal or pendant insertions.
strongest_honest_claim: Every graph on 9 vertices with an isolated vertex is 1-11-representable, but this is already implied by canonical Lemma 2.3(a) together with Theorem 3.5 of the 2025 source.
paper_title_hint: No honest standalone paper title; the current packet collapses to a known isolated-vertex corollary.
candidate_theorem_slice: Every graph on 9 vertices with an isolated vertex is 1-11-representable; therefore any 9-vertex counterexample must have minimum degree at least 1.
next_action: Return to solve on the same target only if the next pass directly compresses the isolate-free 9-vertex residue using the published 2024-2025 tools or a tightly bounded residue census; do not treat the current isolated-vertex slice as new progress.
